reason: handleDoctor .codex branch wiring line `results.push(codexProjectTrustDoctorCheck(projectDir))`. The judgment it dispatches is fully covered in-process by t226; handleDoctor itself is spawn-only (t83 header) and bun --coverage does not measure a spawned subprocess. Seam refactor already applied (#882 precedent, utility.ts:558-590) — this residual wiring line is the second-resort allowlist case per spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii).
expiry: remove when handleDoctor gains an in-process driver test

reason: handleDoctor .codex layer-2 hook-trust reminder label string (FR-2 #1065 relabel to make the two-layer structure explicit). A static pass:true advisory message-string row inside spawn-only handleDoctor — refactor is unnatural per spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii), which names message-string rows as the allowlist case.
expiry: remove when handleDoctor gains an in-process driver test

reason: main() dispatch cases for the grant-standing-delegation / revoke-standing-delegation verbs (#1125). main() is the CLI entry — never driven in-process — so bun --coverage cannot measure its switch arms; the handlers they dispatch (handleGrantStandingDelegation / handleRevokeStandingDelegation) are fully covered in-process by t-standing-grant. Dispatch-case rows are the named second-resort allowlist case in spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii); every pre-existing verb case is unmeasured the same way.
expiry: remove when main() gains an in-process argv driver

reason: Unknown-subcommand usage message string in main()'s default case — the two new verbs were added to the existing single-line list. A spawn-only main() message-string row (spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii)); the surrounding default branch was already unmeasured before this change.
expiry: remove when main() gains an in-process argv driver

reason: Defensive `catch {}` in findActiveStandingGrant that returns null so a verification defect can never wedge a gate (design contract E-SDG: throws collapse to per-gate approval, never fail-open at the check). No natural corpus input throws inside the scan (listIntentDirs / readAllAuditShards / StandingGrant.parse / verifyDelegatedProvenance all swallow their own IO errors), so the catch arm is unreachable by a black-box test; the happy scan and every classification branch are covered in-process by t-standing-grant.
expiry: remove if the scan grows a throw path a test can inject
